diagnosis.primary_leak: No hard veto when wager direction contradicted model projection.
secondary_leaks: Foul and free-throw tail risk was underweighted.
Phoenix comeback pressure and extended starter usage were not modeled strongly enough.
Live team-total entry lacked a minimum edge threshold.
Process allowed a narrative override without recalculating win probability and expected value.
what_worked: Regression signal was partly correct: Connecticut fell from 54 first-half points to 42 second-half points.
Third-quarter pace read was strong: Connecticut scored only 19.
The original model mean of 94-95 was close to the 96-point result.

required_patches.name: Model Direction Veto
required_patches.rule: Do not approve a live wager opposite the model mean unless a documented market-moving input changes the projection by at least 2.5 points.

required_patches.name: Minimum Edge Gate
required_patches.rule: Require projected win probability to exceed sportsbook break-even probability by at least 4 percentage points.

required_patches.name: Late Foul Tail Module
required_patches.rule: Add score-margin, comeback pressure, team-foul state, and end-game free-throw distributions to live totals.

required_patches.name: Blowout Split
required_patches.rule: Separate true clock-kill blowouts from competitive comeback games; large halftime leads do not automatically imply an under.

required_patches.name: Override Labeling
required_patches.rule: Any user override must be logged as PASS/NO-BET by the model unless the recalculated edge clears the gate.
